questions.stem: In a passage about digital banking adoption in India, the author mentions that 'the trajectory of fintech growth has been nothing short of meteoric.' What does the author imply?
A passage discusses climate change policies. The author states governments must balance economic growth with environmental protection. What is the tone of the passage?
Based on a passage about rural employment schemes, which statement can be inferred? The passage mentions MGNREGA provides 100 days of employment and has reduced rural-urban migration.
options: MGNREGA has achieved its objective partially

questions.stem: A passage argues that artificial intelligence will create more jobs than it destroys. Which of the following, if true, would weaken this argument?
options: Most new AI jobs require advanced degrees unavailable to displaced workers

questions.stem: In a passage about Indian banking reforms, the word 'recapitalisation' is used. In context, it most nearly means:
A passage discusses the importance of financial literacy. The author's primary purpose is to:
According to a passage on renewable energy, what is the main challenge in solar energy adoption in India?
options: High initial investment and storage costs

questions.stem: The passage states: 'The pharmaceutical industry walks a tightrope between innovation and affordability.' This means:
options: Balancing R&D costs with accessible pricing is difficult

questions.stem: Based on a passage about e-governance initiatives, which title best suits the passage that discusses how digital platforms reduce corruption and improve service delivery?
A passage discusses microfinance institutions. The antonym of 'proliferation' as used in the passage (meaning rapid spread of MFIs) would be:
The Reserve Bank of India has ___ several measures to boost liquidity in the market during the economic slowdown.
Despite the government's efforts, the ___ between the rich and poor continues to widen in developing nations.
The new policy aims to ___ a sense of responsibility among citizens regarding waste management.
The company's decision to downsize was ___ by falling revenues and increasing competition.
Access to quality healthcare remains ___ in many rural areas, leading to preventable deaths.
The government is ___ to implement reforms that will attract foreign investment without compromising domestic industries.
The pandemic has ___ the need for robust digital infrastructure in educational institutions.
Economists have warned that ___ spending without corresponding revenue generation could lead to fiscal deficit.
The bilateral trade agreement is expected to ___ economic ties between the two nations significantly.
The regulatory body has taken a ___ stance on data privacy, imposing heavy fines on violators.
Find the error: (A) The committee have decided / (B) to postpone the meeting / (C) until further notice / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) Each of the boys / (B) were given / (C) a prize for their performance / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) He is one of those persons / (B) who is always ready / (C) to help others / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) The manager along with his team / (B) are working hard / (C) to meet the deadline / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) Neither the teacher / (B) nor the students / (C) was present / (D) in the class / (E) No error
Find the error: (A) I have been living / (B) in this city / (C) since five years / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) The news / (B) are not true / (C) according to reliable sources / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) He insisted / (B) on me to go / (C) to the market / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) Despite of working hard / (B) he could not / (C) pass the examination / (D) No error
Find the error: (A) No sooner did he reach the station / (B) when the train / (C) had already left / (D) No error

Rearrange: (P) climate change is (Q) that requires immediate (R) a global challenge (S) attention from all nations. Correct order:

Which sentence should come LAST? (A) The bank launched a new savings scheme. (B) It offers attractive interest rates. (C) Many customers have enrolled already. (D) The scheme targets young professionals.
Rearrange: (P) education is the key (Q) to reducing poverty (R) and empowering communities (S) in developing nations. Correct order:

Rearrange: (P) the farmer protests (Q) highlighted the need (R) for comprehensive (S) agricultural reform policies. Correct order:

Which sentence is the SECOND in logical order? (A) Therefore, exports declined sharply. (B) The rupee depreciated against the dollar. (C) Global demand for Indian goods fell. (D) The government intervened with fiscal stimulus.
Rearrange: (P) technological innovation (Q) has transformed (R) the way businesses (S) operate in the modern world. Correct order:
The government's ___ approach to handling the crisis was appreciated by international observers.
The witness gave a ___ account of the events, leaving no detail unmentioned.
The new regulation is expected to ___ unfair trade practices in the market.
His ___ behaviour during the meeting alienated his colleagues and damaged his reputation.
The ___ of evidence against the accused made conviction almost certain.
The two nations signed a ___ agreement to cooperate on counter-terrorism operations.
The CEO's resignation came as a ___ to the board members who had expected him to continue.
The ___ nature of the disease makes early detection extremely difficult.
The report ___ that immediate action is required to prevent further environmental degradation.
The merger will ___ the company's position as the market leader in the sector.

questions.stem: The sceneries of Kashmir are very beautiful. Correct form:

1. It aims to provide affordable healthcare. (P) The scheme covers hospitalisation expenses. (Q) Ayushman Bharat was launched in 2018. (R) Over 50 crore citizens are eligible. (S) It is the world's largest health insurance scheme. Which is the correct sequence?
Arrange in order: (A) Banks began offering digital services. (B) Internet penetration increased in India. (C) UPI revolutionised payment systems. (D) Demonetisation accelerated cashless transactions.

Which sentence starts the paragraph? (A) This led to a sharp fall in oil prices. (B) OPEC nations disagreed on production cuts. (C) Global oil demand fell due to the pandemic. (D) Many oil-dependent economies suffered severely.

Arrange: (P) Consequently, inflation rates rose sharply. (Q) Supply chain disruptions affected global trade. (R) The pandemic shut down manufacturing units worldwide. (S) Central banks raised interest rates to control prices.

Which sentence comes last? (A) The start-up ecosystem in India is booming. (B) Government initiatives like Start-up India have helped. (C) India now has over 100 unicorn companies. (D) Venture capital funding has increased manifold.
Arrange: (P) India's GDP growth slowed in 2020. (Q) The lockdown halted economic activities. (R) Recovery began in the third quarter. (S) The government announced stimulus packages.

Which is the opening sentence? (A) Women's participation in the workforce has improved. (B) Several policies promote gender equality. (C) However, the gap remains significant. (D) Education of girls has increased in recent decades.

Arrange: (P) Electric vehicles are gaining popularity. (Q) Governments offer subsidies for EV purchases. (R) Fossil fuel pollution is a growing concern. (S) Battery technology has improved significantly.

Which sentence comes second? (A) Consequently, many small businesses shut down. (B) The pandemic severely impacted the economy. (C) Government relief packages provided some respite. (D) Unemployment rates hit record highs.

Arrange: (P) Cybersecurity threats have increased globally. (Q) Remote work expanded the attack surface. (R) Companies are investing heavily in security solutions. (S) Data breaches cost businesses billions annually.
What does 'to burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: To work late into the night

questions.stem: What does 'a shot in the dark' mean?
What does 'to turn over a new leaf' mean?
options: To change one's behaviour for the better

questions.stem: What does 'once in a blue moon' mean?
What does 'to pull strings' mean?
What does 'the ball is in your court' mean?
options: It is your turn to take action

questions.stem: What does 'to beat around the bush' mean?
What does 'a blessing in disguise' mean?
options: Something bad that turns out to be good

questions.stem: What does 'to cry over spilt milk' mean?
options: To be upset about wasted milk
To regret something that cannot be undone

questions.stem: What does 'to let the cat out of the bag' mean?
